name: 13p11_NOR_shore_SD
description: Proximal NOR-shore segmental-duplication architecture on chr13p11-p12, immediately distal to the 45S rDNA array (documented in /ribosomal/chr13_NOR.json). The shore region is composed of acrocentric-shared SD modules (DJ/PJ duplicons) of 1-50 kb at 95-99% identity recurring across the five acrocentric short arms (13p, 14p, 15p, 21p, 22p). Distal junction (DJ) anchors the rDNA tail; proximal junction (PJ) bridges to centromeric alpha-satellite. T2T-CHM13 resolves the modules; GRCh38 is essentially gap.

architecture.internal_structure: Distal-junction (DJ) and proximal-junction (PJ) duplicons interleaved with HSat1/HSat3 satellite blocks; modules recurring on all five acrocentric short arms produce inter-chromosomal NAHR (Robertsonian translocations rob(13;14), rob(13;15), rob(13;21)).
